name: CERT-In Cybersecurity Directions
summary: India's CERT-In Directions issued under the Information Technology Act mandate strict cybersecurity incident reporting and data retention requirements for all organizations operating in India. The directions require reporting of cybersecurity incidents within 6 hours and impose log retention obligations of 180 days within Indian jurisdiction.
keyRequirements: Report cybersecurity incidents to CERT-In within 6 hours of noticing or being notified
Maintain ICT system logs for 180 days within Indian jurisdiction
Synchronize ICT system clocks to Network Time Protocol (NTP) servers
Data centres, VPS providers, cloud providers, and VPN providers must register subscriber information for 5 years
Virtual asset service providers must maintain KYC and transaction records for 5 years
Designate a Point of Contact for CERT-In communications
penalties: Non-compliance may result in imprisonment up to one year or fine up to INR 100,000 under the IT Act. CERT-In may issue directions to block, restrict, or mandate specific security measures. Service providers may face license revocation.
